name: Playwright Browser Automation
description: General-purpose browser automation Claude Skill using Playwright. Claude writes and executes custom automation code on-the-fly for any browser task: testing pages, filling forms, taking screenshots, validating responsive design, checking links, and automating login flows. Auto-detects running dev servers. Uses visible browser by default.

findings_summary.notes: High-quality, well-established browser automation skill with MIT license. Very high actual star count (1791) — significantly higher than the 180 recorded, confirming genuine community adoption. SKILL.md is concise and well-structured with clear workflow steps. run.js uses Node.js child_process.execSync only for npm install and npx playwright install — standard package management, not arbitrary command execution. Writes test scripts to /tmp (not the skill directory) — good security practice. Progressive disclosure pattern: concise SKILL.md + optional API_REFERENCE.md. Auto-detect dev servers via port scanning helpers. Lib directory contains utility functions. No hidden hooks. Plugin structure uses .claude-plugin/ metadata. Concern: the skill allows Claude to write and execute arbitrary JavaScript automation code — this is by design and clearly documented. Users should understand they are granting Claude the ability to control their browser. No obfuscation or dangerous exfiltration patterns found in the skill infrastructure itself.
